errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.network: Network connection error. Check your connection.
errors.unauthorized: Session expired. Please log in again.

contractErrors.generic: Contract execution failed. Please try again.

failed.label: Failed
failed.emphasis: The transaction did not go through

dashboard.loadError: We couldn't load your dashboard summary.

bills.pageSubtitle: Manage and track your recurring bills
bills.billCreationDesc: The initiating form should own validation and contract-build feedback. Longer-running submit states should move into a stack that stays visible while the user continues working.
bills.billWarning: This bill request is built as an on-chain USDC payment payload. Your wallet signs and submits the transaction; RemitWise only prepares the payload.

form.repeatDescOff: Leave off for one-time bills, or turn on to add a visible payment schedule.
form.repeatDescOn: {{preview}}; reminders start {{days}} days before each due date.

status.idleDesc: Validation lives at field level, contract-build feedback stays inline above the CTA, and submit progress should move into the persistent stack rail.
status.pendingDesc: Hold the user in this form context until the bill payload is ready for wallet approval.
status.successDesc: The next step should open wallet approval immediately, while a stacked confirmation card remains visible if the user navigates away.
status.errorTitle: Bill request could not be prepared

asyncPanel.title: Bill Submission Pattern
asyncPanel.desc: Recurring bill creation is a good example of why inline build states and persistent submission stacks should be separate surfaces.
asyncPanel.queueDesc: On desktop, anchor stacked confirmation cards near the top-right edge of the main content. On mobile, move the same stack directly below the initiating form or modal footer.
asyncPanel.footer: This pass does not require any Tailwind config changes. It uses existing red focus rings, dark surfaces, and border-opacity utilities already present across the app.

billStages.stage1Detail: Keep field errors attached to the triggering inputs so users do not need to reconcile a toast with the form.
billStages.stage2Detail: The form card should own the build state because the user still needs the bill amount, schedule, and recurring toggle in view.
billStages.stage3Detail: Open a focused confirmation step only after the contract payload succeeds and the user can act immediately.
billStages.stage4Detail: Show network progress in a stacked surface that persists even after the form scrolls away.

billQueue.item1Detail: Primary submission remains expanded until wallet approval or an error resolves.
billQueue.item2Detail: Secondary tasks compress so multiple actions never dominate the screen.
billQueue.item3Detail: Leave the success state visible briefly so the user can trust the outcome without opening history.
